name: SPL CLI Main Entry Point
description: CLI entry point for the SPL (Security Policy Language) interpreter. Parses a policy file and evaluates it against a request JSON file, outputting ALLOW or DENY decisions.

functions.name: main
functions.description: Main CLI function that reads a policy file and request JSON from command-line arguments, parses the policy, verifies it against the request with a hardcoded environment, and prints ALLOW or DENY to stdout.

error_cases.name: PolicyFileNotFound
error_cases.condition: sys.argv[1] does not exist or is not readable

error_cases.name: RequestFileNotFound
error_cases.condition: sys.argv[2] does not exist or is not readable

error_cases.name: InvalidJSON
error_cases.condition: request file does not contain valid JSON

error_cases.name: ParseError
error_cases.condition: policy file contains invalid SPL syntax

error_cases.name: KeyError
error_cases.condition: verify result does not contain 'allow' key

preconditions: sys.argv must contain at least 3 elements (program name + 2 arguments)
sys.argv[1] must be a valid path to a readable .spl file
sys.argv[2] must be a valid path to a readable .json file
The policy file must contain parseable SPL syntax
The request file must contain valid JSON
The verify result must be a dict-like object with an 'allow' key
postconditions: Prints 'ALLOW' or 'DENY' to stdout based on verification result
side_effects: Writes usage message to stderr if argument count < 3
Writes ALLOW or DENY to stdout

structured_side_effects.description: Reads policy source code from file path provided as first CLI argument
Reads request JSON data from file path provided as second CLI argument
Prints usage message to stderr when insufficient arguments provided
Prints ALLOW or DENY decision to stdout

invariants: Environment dictionary has fixed structure with 'vars', 'now', 'per_day_count', and 'crypto' keys
All crypto validation lambdas (dpop_ok, merkle_ok, vrf_ok, thresh_ok) always return True

data_access.rationale: Reads policy and request files from filesystem, writes verification results to stdout and error messages to stderr

side_effects.rationale: Reads SPL policy source and JSON request data from files specified in command-line arguments
Writes usage information to stderr on invalid invocation
Terminates process with exit code 1 when insufficient arguments provided
